evals.prompt: I want to create an MCP server for my internal Notion workspace. We have pages, databases, and team wikis. I want tools to search pages, create new pages, and update existing ones. Walk me through creating it with the mcpheroctl CLI step by step.
evals.expected_output: A step-by-step walkthrough of the full wizard pipeline using mcpheroctl: create-session, conversation loop until is_ready, start, list/refine/submit tools, env vars, set-auth, generate-code, deploy. Should include actual commands with placeholder values and explain the polling pattern for async steps.

assertions.text: Explains iterating wizard conversation until is_ready: true
Mentions polling 'wizard state' after async steps like start and generate-code
Shows --tool-id flags on wizard submit-tools command
Shows --var UUID=VALUE format for wizard submit-env-vars
Mentions server_url and bearer_token returned from wizard deploy

evals.prompt: I want to build an MCP server that wraps our internal REST API (base URL: https://internal.mycompany.com/api, needs an Authorization header with a Bearer token). What tools should I create and how do I go through the wizard?
evals.expected_output: Guidance on using wizard conversation to describe the API, iterating until is_ready, then going through start, list-tools, submit-tools, list-env-vars, submit-env-vars, set-auth, generate-code, deploy. Should mention the polling pattern for async steps.

assertions.text: Explains iterating wizard conversation until is_ready: true
Correctly identifies async steps (start, generate-code) requiring polling
Explains calling wizard state and checking processing_status
Mentions env vars will be suggested for the API base URL and bearer token credential
Mentions server_url and bearer_token returned from wizard deploy

evals.prompt: How do I connect my newly deployed MCPHero server to Claude Desktop? The server_url is https://api.mcphero.app/mcp/abc123/mcp and the bearer_token is sk-abc123xyz.
evals.expected_output: Instructions to add the server to claude_desktop_config.json with the correct JSON structure using the provided server_url and bearer_token.

assertions.text: Mentions claude_desktop_config.json or Library/Application Support/Claude path
Shows the mcpServers JSON block with a url field containing the provided server URL
Includes the bearer token (sk-abc123xyz) in the config as an Authorization header
Uses the exact provided URL in the example
